dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 9 for a breathing problem on Willow Street, [CITY]. Patient reported symptoms of throat tightness and trouble breathing.
Hi, I'm Jordan from EMS. How long have you been having these symptoms?
It's my throat. It's itchy and tight. I can't catch my breath properly. It started last night.
Can you tell me where you are now?
Open your eyes for me, please.
What day is it today, sir?
Could you squeeze my finger for me?
Let me confirm your airway. It's patent and self-maintained.
I can see some mild labored breathing. Are you feeling short of breath now?
There's a strong radial pulse, regular and consistent with no irregularities noted.
You mentioned a tight and sore throat. Is there pain associated with it?
What pre-existing medical conditions do you have?
Pulse is 70. Strong and regular.
Respirations at 20, mild laboring.
Blood pressure is 138 over 90. Stable so far.
Oxygen saturation is 100%, on room air.
The EKG shows sinus rhythm, no changes noted.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Paramedic
dialogue.utterance: Let me assess your throat. You have some hoarseness and stridor present. No pain reported, just tightness.
Let me listen to your lungs. Upper lobes clear, some diminished in the bases. Trachea is midline. You can hear stridor.
Blood glucose is 108, well within normal range.
Let me place ECG pads on your chest. Per standing order.
Four lead ECG placed successfully and shows normal findings.
You're going to receive DuoNeb through a nebulizer-that's Albuterol and Ipratropium. All right with that?
Starting the nebulizer now. Let's see if that helps your breathing.
Per protocol, we'll administer Dexamethasone IV next. Also Benadryl, per allergic reaction protocol.
I'll give you 10 mg Dexamethasone IV. I've placed an IV in your extremity vein.
Reran EKG. Still shows normal sinus rhythm.
Blood pressure is slightly reduced at 126/79 but remains in normal range.
Pulse is 87 now, respirations steady at 18.
Oxygen level dropped a little to 96%. Will continue to monitor.
Next step is a low dose of Epi at 0.3 mg IM. Is that okay to proceed?
Blood glucose was confirmed at 109. No issues.
Now administering 50 mg of Benadryl IV to control symptoms.
We'll be heading to [COMPANY_NAME]. Best for your breathing issue.
You can sit up now to assist loading to the stretcher.
Securing you to the stretcher. All set for transport.
Breathing rate is 16 but still slightly labored.
Pulse is still at 87, not elevated further.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Paramedic
dialogue.utterance: Exam confirms your hoarse voice and stridor. Trachea remains midline. Tongue slightly swollen.
Your symptoms began slowly and progressively worsened into the morning?
Have you experienced any pain with the tightness in your throat?
Has the tightness in your throat become more severe recently today compared to last night?
Confirmed that blood sugar is now 109.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Paramedic
dialogue.utterance: Stridor is heard on inspiration, consistent with upper airway compromise.
You have a history of asthma and COPD. Also, note on medical history of HIV. Are you on treatment for those?
Lung sounds in central and upper lobes remain clear and no signs of further compromise.
Blood pressure is 130/80. Still normal.
Breathing pattern continues at 16, not deeply labored.
We are en route to [COMPANY_NAME].
Please let me know if you have trouble with breathing or swallowing during transport.
Pulse is steady at 80, still regular and strong.
Patient remains alert and oriented to person.
Oxygen saturation is 100%. No decline observed so far.
Approximately 10 minutes of transport have passed. Let me double check your throat condition.
Blood glucose is still at 109. No changes noted.
Breathing rate remains 16 with some effort lower in the chest.
Oxygen remains 100%. No new concerns.
No further action needed. Preparing for hospital handoff.
Patient information is uploaded to the hospital EMR system. Handoff to hospital staff is ready.
Alert hospital staff about a short PR interval on 12-lead as found for a 40+ male.
Include destination hospital of [CITY] and the medications: Albuterol, Ipratropium, Dexamethasone, Benadryl, and Epi dosages and times.
Patient denies pain and is fully oriented to person.
Patient is HIV positive, asymptomatic, and has no known allergies.
Airway remains open and no difficulty maintaining it.
Hospital ER is now accessible and ready to receive patient in stable condition.
Clinical impression is allergic reaction and acute bronchospasm.
No further medications required. All previous interventions stable.
No signs of worsening condition. Continue transport as planned.
Did you take any medications for your symptoms before we arrived?
No new procedures administered. Continue to monitor as needed.
We will continue the IV and ECG monitoring en route.
I will adjust the stretcher to ensure comfort and safety during transport.
The patient is alert and answering correctly. Mentation is A&OX4.
I will confirm oxygen flow and IV site during the remainder of transport.
No complications detected so far.
Patient is stable and managing his own airway well.
Hospital is in sight. Let me stabilize the stretcher for the last leg.
Airway remains patent and patient is not in distress.
Wheeling in the patient now. Ready to transfer to hospital staff.
Rechecked glucose, remains at 109. No action required.
Oxygen is hanging steady at 100%. Continue monitoring.
No alterations are occurring. Need to maintain current course.
Continue to monitor breathing effort en route.
Maintain current medications and monitoring.
Patient itching resolved per medications given.
Transport is uneventful and within protocol guidelines.
No further actions are indicated at this time.
Did you experience any pain today with this issue?

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Paramedic
dialogue.utterance: No signs of increasing swelling. Throat condition is same as at origin.
No pain reported recently, patient denies any significant discomfort.
No action is needed for pain scale.
GCS is 15. Patient is alert, oriented, and comfortable.
Eye opening, verbal response, motor control all normal.
Patient is not in distress at all. All signs are normal.
Alert, oriented times 4. No signs of altered mental status.
Confirmed mental status is intact. A&OX4.
No changes from initial observation about mental acuity.
I will now log this into the system as GCS 15 and fully alert.
Confirmed on medical chart that pain score is 0 today.
No signs of pain or discomfort reported during entire assessment.
